note: Module 7 — not a list of missing numbers, but the unknowns that would actually flip a judgment. Ordered by decision weight.

gaps.unknown: Customer pull — do a specialty foundry's actual accounts want SEA capacity or SEA-based support?
gaps.would_change: This flips whether ANY non-Singapore move is warranted at all. It is the single highest-weight unknown.
gaps.why_open: Requires internal design-win / demand data; not available to an external consultant.
gaps.how_to_close: Customer sourcing mandates, design-win geography, public China+1 supplier disclosures as proxy.

gaps.unknown: China+1 durability — is the diversification structural or cyclical?
gaps.would_change: Flips the urgency and the option value of a SEA back-end/optionality play.
gaps.how_to_close: Track export-control/tariff trajectory and named customer sourcing shifts (analyst + filings).

gaps.unknown: Competitor forward capacity in Singapore (GF, VIS–NXP) — ramp, node mix, utilization.
gaps.would_change: Flips crowding risk for mature-node capacity in the regional anchor.
gaps.why_open: Announced but ramp details not public.
gaps.how_to_close: Company filings, capex disclosures, node roadmaps.

gaps.unknown: Site-level firm power availability (hundreds of MW, 24/7).
gaps.would_change: Flips feasibility of any back-end or front-end placement, far more than electricity price does.
gaps.how_to_close: TNB / MEA-PEA / PLN / EVN / Meralco large-user data; site-level power-quality studies.

gaps.unknown: Semiconductor-specific engineer pool and poachability (absolute, deployable).
gaps.would_change: Flips talent feasibility; graduate percentages do not.
gaps.why_open: Public data mixes actuals, enrollment, and policy targets.
gaps.how_to_close: Industry-association headcounts; ministry yearbooks; recruiter salary guides by role.

gaps.unknown: Materials localization — quantitative import-dependence by material.
gaps.why_open: No unified public quantitative data; currently author judgment (D).
gaps.how_to_close: SEMI material reports, customs/import data, named local-supplier lists.
